title: Mega Blastoise Battle Guide
summary: A [Water] special [tank] whose [mega-launcher] boosts every pulse move by 50%, turning [water-pulse], [aura-sphere], and [dark-pulse] into STAB-tier damage from one slot.
strengths: 120/115 mixed bulk [absorb]s switches from both sides
135 SpA gives real offensive teeth unusual for a [tank]
weaknesses: Speed 78 moves second against almost every attacker
Pre-Mega turn lacks [mega-launcher] and can be exploited
bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Blastoise is a [Water]-type special [tank] that weaponises [mega-launcher] to boost every pulse move by 50%. With 120/115 mixed bulk and 135 SpA, it keeps the defensive identity of the base form while gaining real wallbreaker offense. Speed 78 is unchanged, so the plan is still switch in, fire a pulse, and grind opponents down across multiple exchanges.

## Strengths
[mega-launcher] turns [water-pulse] into a functionally 90 BP STAB with flinch utility, while [aura-sphere] and [dark-pulse] share the same 50% boost from one slot. Three of four attacks fire at near-STAB damage, which is absurdly efficient for a [tank]. 120/115 bulk lets it switch into special attacks without committing a trade, and 135 SpA is genuine enough that defensive switch-ins can't ignore the attack.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Speed 78 is the hard cap — every relevant attacker moves first, so the Tera timing must be planned a[round] one telegraphed attack. [Grass] and [Electric] remain 2x after Mega Evolution, which means any competent [Grass] or [Electric] attacker still forces it out. The pre-Mega turn runs [torrent] instead of [mega-launcher], so a predicted neutral hit can do more damage than expected. Always Mega Evolve into a predicted switch.

## Recommended Moveset
[water-pulse] is locked as the main STAB because [mega-launcher] turns it into the most efficient slot on the set. [aura-sphere] is the secondary cove[rage] for [Dark], [Steel], and [Normal] walls, and also receives the launcher boost. [dark-pulse] handles [Psychic] and [Ghost] threats — especially Mega Alakazam and Mega Gengar, both of which would otherwise ignore this set entirely. The last slot is [ice-beam] for [Grass], [Dragon], and [Flying] cove[rage].

## Item · Ability · Tera
[blastoisinite] is the only item because the Mega Stone is mandatory. [mega-launcher] is locked after the evolution. Tera [Water] maxes [water-pulse] damage, while Tera [Fairy] flips the [Grass], [Dragon], and [Dark] weaknesses in one move. Tera [Fighting] is a rarer alternative that boosts [aura-sphere] into a finisher at the cost of defensive flexibility.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair Mega Blastoise with a physical wall — [Steel] or [Ground] — to cover the other side of the board, and bring a [Ground] or [Dragon] partner for the [Electric] weakness. Trick Room users and priority attackers help compensate for the Speed cap, and [volt-switch] or [u-turn] pivots bring it in safely. A cleaner behind the [tank] capitalises on the chip its pulses leave behind.

## Play Patterns
Mega Evolve into a predicted switch, then keep [water-pulse] pressure up while rotating [aura-sphere] and [dark-pulse] against matchup-specific threats. Save [ice-beam] for the [Grass] or [Dragon] pivot, and Tera the moment an [Electric] or [Grass] move is telegraphed.
